name: Bottom-Up DP
summary: dp[i] is true when the first i characters split into dictionary words; extend any reachable prefix by a word that follows it.
visualizationIntent: init: draw a reachability cell for every cut position 0..n; dp[0] = 1 (empty prefix is reachable), the rest 0. loop: pointer i marks the prefix end being decided, pointer j scans every earlier cut. check: dp[j] (blue) is the reachable prefix we rely on and the readout shows the piece s[j:i] and whether it is a dictionary word. update: when a reachable prefix is followed by a real word, dp[i] flips to 1 and flashes. return: dp[n] turns green if the whole string is segmentable, red if not.

lineExplanations.3: Put the dictionary into a set so word lookups are instant.
lineExplanations.4: Measure the length of the string.
lineExplanations.5: Create a reachability flag for every cut position 0..n, all starting false (0).
lineExplanations.6: Base case: the empty prefix (length 0) is always reachable.
lineExplanations.7: Decide reachability for every prefix length i from 1 up to n.
lineExplanations.8: Try every earlier cut j as the start of the last word ending at i.
lineExplanations.9: Take the substring piece from cut j to cut i — the candidate last word.
lineExplanations.10: Check whether that piece is a word in the dictionary.
lineExplanations.11: If the prefix ending at j is reachable AND the piece is a real word, then i is reachable too.
lineExplanations.12: Mark prefix length i as reachable.
lineExplanations.13: The whole string is segmentable exactly when the full length n is reachable.
lineExplanations.14: Return whether the string can be segmented into dictionary words.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def wordBreak(self, s, wordDict): defines the method receiving the string and dictionary.
syntaxExplanations.3: set(wordDict) builds a set for O(1) membership tests.
syntaxExplanations.4: len(s) returns the number of characters in s.
syntaxExplanations.5: [0] * (n + 1) builds a list of n+1 zeros (all 'not reachable').
syntaxExplanations.6: dp[0] = 1 marks the empty prefix reachable.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(1, n + 1): loops i over 1, 2, ..., n.
syntaxExplanations.8: for j in range(i): loops j over 0, 1, ..., i-1.
syntaxExplanations.9: s[j:i] slices the substring between cut j and cut i.
syntaxExplanations.10: piece in words is a boolean: is the piece a dictionary word?
syntaxExplanations.11: if dp[j] == 1 and fits: both the prefix must be reachable and the piece a word.
syntaxExplanations.12: dp[i] = 1 marks prefix length i as reachable.
syntaxExplanations.13: dp[n] == 1 is true when the full string is reachable.
syntaxExplanations.14: return result hands the boolean answer back to the caller.
